commentaries.author: Cyprian of Carthage
commentaries.content: And once more: "Whosoever shall lose his life for my sake, the same shall save it."

commentaries.author: Cyril of Alexandria
commentaries.content: Even though one has wealth and abundance of possessions, yet what profit has he from them when he has lost himself? Treasures profit not the wicked, but the fashion of this world passes away; and like clouds those pleasures recede, and riches fly away from those that possess them. But righteousness delivers from death. Commentary on Luke, Homily

commentaries.author: Cyril of Alexandria
commentaries.content: What fear, therefore, can the saints now feel, if that which seemed to be hard proves rather joyous to them that bear it. Commentary on Luke, Homily

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: "Whosoever "says He, "will save his life, shall lose it; and whosoever will lose his life for my sake, the same shall save it."

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
